reasoning_model_1: The model fails significantly on content accuracy. The generated audio cuts off abruptly at 00:03, producing only the text 'Finally finished, I can finally', completely omitting the phrase 'breathe a sigh of relief.' Because the content is incomplete and inconsistent with the script, and no non-linguistic sigh is audible in the generated segment, this audio is rated 0 based on the global considerations for incomplete/poor content.
reasoning_model_2: The model performs excellently. It accurately reproduces the full text script without errors (00:00-00:06). Crucially, it effectively incorporates the required paralinguistic feature: a clear, audible sigh ('Ahhh') is inserted at approximately 00:02, right after the phrase 'Finally finished.' The prosody is natural, and the tone effectively conveys the sense of relief requested. The non-linguistic vocalization acts as a perfect bridge between the two clauses.
model_comparison: Model 1 failed to generate the complete sentence, cutting off midway, which results in an automatic failure according to the evaluation criteria. It also failed to demonstrate the requested sigh before the cutoff. Model 2, in contrast, followed all instructions perfectly. It generated the full text fluently and successfully integrated a natural-sounding sigh at a logical point in the sentence (00:02), enhancing the semantic meaning of the text. Therefore, Model 2 is the clear winner.
